instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Which Deaths are Not Covered by Term Insurance Policy?
Meta description: A term insurance plan doesn’t cover all kinds of deaths. If the death occurs under certain specific circumstances, the insurer can reject the death claim.
Keywords: term insurance cover, term cover, online term plan, term plan premium calculator
A term insurance policy is one of the most cost-effective ways through which you can secure your family’s future. It provides some much-needed financial assistance to the nominees of a policyholder upon the said policyholder’s death. The payout that the nominees receive can be used by them to further their life goals as they see fit.
That said, not all deaths are covered by a term insurance plan. As a matter of fact, there are certain situations where an insurance service provider can end up rejecting the death claim filed by the nominees. Wondering what kinds of death are out of the purview of a term insurance cover? Continue reading to find out. But before we go ahead, let’s quickly take a look at the concept of death claim.
What is a Death Claim?
In the event of the policyholder’s death during the policy term, the nominees of the policyholder are required to make a formal request to the insurance company for the disbursal of the death benefit as applicable under the plan. This request that the nominees make is termed as a death claim.
However, before disbursing the death benefit payout, the insurance company usually verifies the veracity of the death claim. And during this process of verification, if the insurer finds any discrepancies or mistakes, the death claim may be rejected and the payout will not be made to the nominees.
Kinds of Death That Are Not Covered by a Term Insurance Policy
The death claim may be rejected by the insurance service provider if the policyholder’s death is not covered under the term insurance plan. Here’s a quick overview of the kinds of death that a term cover doesn’t provide for.
Death by suicide
In the event of the policyholder’s death due to suicide or due to self-inflicted injuries, insurance companies will reject the death claim and not pay out the term insurance cover to the nominees.
Death resulting from intoxication or the consumption of drugs or alcohol
If the policyholder dies directly or indirectly due to the consumption of intoxicating substances like drugs or alcohol, the insurance provider will not entertain any claims. For instance, if the policyholder dies in an accident, where they’ve been found to have driven the vehicle under the influence of any intoxicating substances, the insurance company will not honour any claims.
Death as a result of involvement in criminal or malicious activities
If the policyholder, at the time of death, has been found to be involved in any criminal, malicious, or detrimental activities in any way, the insurance provider has the right to reject the death claim and not pay the term cover to the nominees.
Death due to the involvement in adventure sports
In the event of the policyholder’s death during their participation in adventure sports like skydiving, paragliding, bungee jumping, and others, a term plan will not disburse the death benefit to the nominees.
Death due to a natural calamity
If the policyholder’s death occurs due to an act of god such as an earthquake, tsunami, flood, landslide, storm, or any other kind of natural calamity, such death will not be covered either by offline or online term plans. The death claim in such a case will not be honoured by the insurance company.
Death due to a murder committed by the nominee
If the policyholder’s death has been found to have been committed, planned, or caused directly or indirectly, by the nominee listed under the term plan, the insurance company is well within their right to reject the death claim and not pay the sum assured under the plan.
However, if the death of the policyholder occurred due to a murder committed by a stranger or any other person not connected with the nominee, then such a death would be covered by the term policy.
Death due to undisclosed health problems
At the time of purchase of an offline or online term plan, you’re required to list out all the pre-existing conditions, diseases, ailments, and illnesses that you have. Failing to do so can lead to the death claim being rejected by the insurance provider, especially if the death has anything to do with undisclosed health problems.
Also, if the policyholder has a habit of smoking and fails to disclose it at the time of purchase, the term cover will not be paid out to the nominees of the policyholder even if the death has nothing to do with the smoking habit of the individual.
Conclusion
There you have it. The different kinds of death that are not covered by a typical term insurance policy. That said, before you go ahead and purchase a term plan, make sure to read through the exclusions to get a better idea of what’s not covered. Also, make sure to use a term plan premium calculator to determine the amount of premium that you will have to pay towards the plan.
